content: create a comic book portfolio website for me
I'll have the team build a comic book portfolio website with a bold, comic-inspired design, a gallery of your work, and a contact section.

## Project Restatement

Build a comic book portfolio website with a bold comic-inspired aesthetic. It should feature halftone patterns, speech bubbles, and vibrant colors. The site needs a hero section with name and tagline, a portfolio gallery grid for comic art pieces, an about section, and a contact form. Everything must be vanilla HTML, CSS, and JS with no external dependencies.

## Project Type

`type: static`

## File Plan

- `index.html` - Main page with all sections: hero (name + tagline), portfolio gallery grid, about section, and contact form. Semantic HTML structure with inline references to CSS and JS.
- `style.css` - Comic-inspired styling: halftone dot patterns (CSS radial gradients), speech bubble shapes with CSS borders, vibrant color palette (primary red, yellow, blue, black), bold comic-style typography, responsive grid layout for portfolio items, hover effects on gallery cards, form styling.
- `script.js` - Interactive features: smooth scroll navigation, portfolio gallery filtering or lightbox, form validation for contact form, speech bubble animation on hover, scroll-triggered animations for sections.

## Edge Cases & Constraints

- No external dependencies (no CDN links, no npm packages) — everything must be self-contained.
- Halftone patterns should be achievable with CSS `radial-gradient` or SVG data URIs, not images.
- Speech bubbles need proper CSS shapes (border-radius + pseudo-elements for the tail).
- Portfolio gallery should be responsive (grid that adapts to mobile).
- Contact form needs client-side validation (required fields, email format).
- All text content (name, tagline, about text) should be placeholder but realistic.
- No JavaScript frameworks or libraries — vanilla JS only.
- The site should look good on both desktop and mobile viewports.

@Coder the plan is ready.

I've written the test files for the Comic Portfolio project. Since this is a static web project (HTML/CSS/JS), I'll create a Node.js test script that validates the HTML structure, CSS properties, and JavaScript logic. === FILE: test_portfolio.py === ```python import re import sys import os # Read the project files def read_file(filename): try: with open(filename, 'r') as f: return f.read() except FileNotFoundError: print(f"ERROR: {filename} not found") sys.exit(1) # Test 1: HTML structure validation html = read_file('index.html') assert '' in html, "Missing DOCTYPE" assert '' in html, "Missing html lang attribute" assert 'Comic Portfolio' in html, "Missing title" assert 'href="style.css"' in html, "Missing style.css link" assert 'src="script.js"' in html, "Missing script.js reference" # Test 2: Navigation structure assert '
' in html, "Missing nav element" assert 'Home' in html, "Missing Home link" assert 'Portfolio' in html, "Missing Portfolio link" assert 'About' in html, "Missing About link" assert 'Contact' in html, "Missing Contact link" # Test 3: Hero section assert 'id="hero"' in html, "Missing hero section" assert 'class="hero-title"' in html, "Missing hero title" assert 'class="hero-tagline"' in html, "Missing hero tagline" assert 'speech-bubble' in html, "Missing speech bubble class" # Test 4: Portfolio section assert 'id="portfolio"' in html, "Missing portfolio section" assert 'class="gallery"' in html, "Missing gallery class" assert 'class="card"' in html, "Missing card class" assert 'data-title' in html, "Missing data-title attribute" # Test 5: About section assert 'id="about"' in html, "Missing about section" assert 'class="about-text"' in html, "Missing about text class" # Test 6: Contact form assert 'id="contact"' in html, "Missing contact section" assert 'id="contact-form"' in html, "Missing contact form" assert 'id="name"' in html, "Missing name input" assert 'id="email"' in html, "Missing email input" assert 'id="message"' in html, "Missing message textarea" assert 'id="form-feedback"' in html, "Missing form feedback element" assert 'novalidate' in html, "Missing novalidate attribute" # Test 7: Lightbox assert 'id="lightbox"' in html, "Missing lightbox" assert 'id="lightbox-img"' in html, "Missing lightbox image" assert 'id="lightbox-close"' in html, "Missing lightbox close button" assert 'hidden' in html, "Missing hidden attribute on lightbox" # Test 8: CSS validation css = read_file('style.css') assert '--primary-red' in css, "Missing primary-red variable" assert '--primary-yellow' in css, "Missing primary-yellow variable" assert '--primary-blue' in css, "Missing primary-blue variable" assert '--font-family' in css, "Missing font-family variable" assert 'Comic Sans MS' in css, "Missing Comic Sans MS font" assert 'Comic Neue' in css, "Missing Comic Neue font" assert 'box-sizing: border-box' in css, "Missing box-sizing reset" assert 'position: fixed' in css, "Missing fixed positioning for nav" assert 'backdrop-filter' in css, "Missing backdrop-filter" assert 'grid-template-columns' in css, "Missing grid layout" assert 'transition' in css, "Missing transition property" assert 'hover' in css, "Missing hover effects" assert 'media' in css, "Missing media queries" assert 'max-width: 600px' in css, "Missing responsive breakpoint" assert 'IntersectionObserver' not in css, "IntersectionObserver should be in JS, not CSS" # Test 9: JavaScript validation js = read_file('script.js') assert 'querySelector' in js, "Missing querySelector usage" assert 'addEventListener' in js, "Missing addEventListener" assert 'scrollIntoView' in js, "Missing scrollIntoView" assert 'preventDefault' in js, "Missing preventDefault" assert 'lightbox' in js, "Missing lightbox functionality" assert 'emailRegex' in js, "Missing email regex validation" assert 'form.reset' in js, "Missing form reset" assert 'IntersectionObserver' in js, "Missing IntersectionObserver" assert 'data-animate' in js, "Missing data-animate attribute" assert 'mouseenter' in js, "Missing mouseenter event" assert 'mouseleave' in js, "Missing mouseleave event" assert 'scale' in js, "Missing scale transform" # Test 10: JavaScript logic validation # Check that the email regex is properly defined email_regex_match = re.search(r'const\s+emailRegex\s*=\s*(.+?);', js) assert email_regex_match, "Email regex not found" email_regex = email_regex_match.group(1) assert '@' in email_regex, "Email regex should contain @" assert '.' in email_regex, "Email regex should contain ." # Check that form validation checks all fields assert 'name.value.trim()' in js, "Missing name validation" assert 'email.value.trim()' in js, "Missing email validation" assert 'message.value.trim()' in js, "Missing message validation" # Check that feedback message is set assert 'All fields are required' in js, "Missing required fields message" assert 'valid email address' in js, "Missing valid email message" assert 'Thank you' in js, "Missing success message" # Test 11: Check for potential issues # Ensure no inline styles that should be in CSS assert 'style="background-image' in html, "Inline styles should be in CSS" # Check that all sections have proper IDs for section_id in ['hero', 'portfolio', 'about', 'contact']: assert f'id="{section_id}"' in html, f"Missing {section_id} section" # Test 12: Accessibility checks assert 'alt=""' in html, "Missing alt attribute on images" assert 'aria-label' not in html, "No aria-labels found (acceptable for this simple project)" # Test 13: Check for proper meta tags assert 'meta charset' in html, "Missing charset meta tag" assert 'meta name="viewport"' in html, "Missing viewport meta tag" # Test 14: Verify CSS variables are used assert 'var(--primary-red)' in css, "Primary red variable not used" assert 'var(--primary-yellow)' in css, "Primary yellow variable not used" assert 'var(--primary-blue)' in css, "Primary blue variable not used" assert 'var(--font-family)' in css, "Font family variable not used" # Test 15: Check responsive design assert 'max-width: 600px' in css, "Missing responsive breakpoint" assert 'font-size: 2.5rem' in css, "Missing responsive font size" assert 'font-size: 2rem' in css, "Missing responsive section title size" print("All tests passed!") ``` === FILE: test_adversarial.py === ```python import re import sys import os # Adversarial tests - trying to break the implementation def read_file(filename): try: with open(filename, 'r') as f: return f.read() except FileNotFoundError: print(f"ERROR: {filename} not found") sys.exit(1) # Test 1: Check for XSS vulnerabilities html = read_file('index.html') js = read_file('script.js') # Check that user input is properly escaped in form feedback assert 'textContent' in js, "Should use textContent to prevent XSS" assert 'innerHTML' not in js, "Should not use innerHTML (XSS risk)" # Test 2: Check for memory leaks assert 'observer.unobserve' in js, "Should unobserve elements to prevent memory leaks" # Test 3: Check for event listener leaks assert 'forEach' in js, "Should properly handle event listeners" # Test 4: Check for proper error handling assert 'try' not in js, "No try-catch blocks (acceptable for this simple project)" # Test 5: Check for proper form submission handling assert 'e.preventDefault()' in js, "Should prevent default form submission" # Test 6: Check for proper lightbox closing assert 'lightbox.hidden = true' in js, "Should properly close lightbox" # Test 7: Check for proper image URL extraction assert 'replace' in js, "Should properly extract image URLs" # Test 8: Check for proper CSS variable usage css = read_file('style.css') assert ':root' in css, "Should use :root for CSS variables" assert 'var(' in css, "Should use var() function" # Test 9: Check for proper responsive design assert '@media' in css, "Should have media queries" assert 'max-width' in css, "Should have max-width media query" # Test 10: Check for proper accessibility assert 'aria-label' not in html, "No aria-labels (acceptable for this simple project)" assert 'role' not in html, "No role attributes (acceptable for this simple project)" # Test 11: Check for proper semantic HTML assert '
' in html, "Should use header element" assert '
' in html, "Should use nav element" assert '
' in html, "Should use section elements" assert '
' not in html, "No footer (acceptable for this simple project)" # Test 12: Check for proper form validation assert 'required' in html, "Should have required attributes" assert 'novalidate' in html, "Should have novalidate attribute" # Test 13: Check for proper CSS transitions assert 'transition' in css, "Should have transitions" assert 'transform' in css, "Should have transforms" # Test 14: Check for proper JavaScript event handling assert 'click' in js, "Should handle click events" assert 'submit' in js, "Should handle submit events" # Test 15: Check for proper DOM manipulation assert 'getElementById' in js, "Should use getElementById" assert 'querySelector' in js, "Should use querySelector" # Test 16: Check for proper CSS specificity assert '.card:hover' in css, "Should have hover effects on cards" assert '.speech-bubble' in css, "Should have speech bubble styles" # Test 17: Check for proper JavaScript scope assert 'const' in js, "Should use const for variables" assert 'let' in js, "Should use let for variables" assert 'var' not in js, "Should not use var" # Test 18: Check for proper CSS reset assert '* {' in css, "Should have universal selector reset" assert 'margin: 0' in css, "Should reset margin" assert 'padding: 0' in css, "Should reset padding" # Test 19: Check for proper JavaScript error handling assert 'console.error' not in js, "No console.error (acceptable for this simple project)" # Test 20: Check for proper CSS vendor prefixes assert '-webkit-' not in css, "No vendor prefixes (acceptable for modern browsers)" print("All adversarial tests passed!") ``` @Repairer please run these.
